message:
fix(bubbletea): improve ANSI sequence prefix detection for partial reads

Addresses race conditions when reading terminal input where ANSI escape
sequences may arrive split across multiple read() calls. Previously,
partial sequences could be misinterpreted as single keypresses.

Changes:

1. key.rs: Add is_sequence_prefix() function that checks if input bytes
   form the prefix of any known ANSI sequence (e.g., "\x1b[" awaiting
   the terminating character)

2. program.rs:
   - Change can_have_more_data to always be true when not at EOF,
     rather than using heuristic n == buf.len()
   - Pass can_have_more_data to parse_key_sequence()
   - In parse_key_sequence(), return NeedMore when input is a known
     sequence prefix and more data may arrive

This ensures that typing rapidly or when the terminal buffers input
doesn't produce spurious escape key events when the sequence is still
being transmitted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
